steps.title: List the eliminated players
steps.content: This is faster as a table fit than as a long exponential setup. In round 1, half of 240 players are eliminated, so $p=120$. Then the eliminated counts keep halving: round 2 has 60, round 3 has 30, and round 4 has 15. Put those four points into Desmos.

steps.title: Read the exponential model
steps.content: The custom regression gives $a=240$ and $b=0.5$. Since Desmos is fitting $y=a b^x$, this means $$p=240(0.5)^r,$$ which is the same as $$p=240\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^r.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice C has the fitted coefficient $240$ and the halving factor $\frac{1}{2}$. Choice A starts with 15 instead of 240. Choices B and D use a factor of $2$, which grows each round instead of shrinking. Choice C is correct.
